Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

I do not think it is a reasonable solution, because radix_tree_gang_lookup() use the given @hash to locate the page, then the value range in the page should covers the given @hash. Why the start value in the page is not zero, and even if zero is special value, we still needs to check other non-zero case, but NOT skip all.

Patch Set 5:

Per Andrea's comment on LU-4472, that hash(0) should share hash(1) bucket, returning a different bucket would result in a bad page (which it appears is what is happening given the errors resulting).  So I've adjusted the ASSERT instead.

parents.subject: LU-3531 llite: fix "lfs getdirstripe" to show stripe info

commit.subject: LU-4472 mdc: Fix mdc_page_locate ASSERT
commit.message:
LU-4472 mdc: Fix mdc_page_locate ASSERT

Storing hash 0 at same as hash 1 should be okay, don't ASSERT in this
case.

Signed-off-by: Nathaniel Clark <nathaniel.l.clark@intel.com>
Change-Id: I9b9488e5374d22dcfc9e7f2c969da3b02778097a
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/8821
Tested-by: Jenkins
